prompt:
A researcher claims that library visits in the downtown branch increased steadily from 2018 to 2022.

Which choice best uses data from the table to support the researcher's claim?

choices.text: In some years the value moved down and in others up, rather than in a single direction.
choices.rationale: Every year moves the same direction, so this misreads the table.

choices.text: The value held steady near 48 thousands for most of the span before a single change late in 2022.
choices.rationale: The table shows the value changing every year, not holding steady.

choices.text: From 2018 to 2022, the value climbed every year, going from 48 to 57 thousands.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across every year shown.

choices.text: It peaked in 2021 at 54 thousands and then reversed course for the rest of the period.
choices.rationale: The largest value is at an endpoint, not the middle year.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Library visits in the downtown branch (thousands)
